channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Michigan, Ohio State players brawl after Wolverines beat No. 2 Buckeyes. Police use pepper spray
item.description: COLUMBUS, Ohio (AP) — A fight broke out at midfield after Michigan stunned No. 2 Ohio State 13-10 on Saturday when Wolverines players attempted to plant their flag on the OSU logo and were confronted by the Buckeyes.
item.dc:creator: MITCH STACY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Cal’s Mendoza a late scratch as Bears get blitzed 38-6 by No. 9 SMU
item.description: Bears trailed 21-0 at halftime and couldn't generate much offense with their starting quarterback not playing due to illness.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Faraudo, The Mercury News

item.title: Norris defies orders to help Piastri and Verstappen loses the Qatar pole to Russell
item.description: LUSAIL, Qatar (AP) — Lando Norris ignored team orders and handed his McLaren teammate Oscar Piastri the sprint race in Qatar on Saturday, while Formula 1 champion Max Verstappen was stripped of the pole position. His penalty elevated George Russell to first on the grid.
item.dc:creator: JAMES ELLINGWORTH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Michigan upsets No. 2 Ohio State 13-10 for Wolverines' 4th straight win over bitter rival
item.description: Dominic Zvada kicked a 21-yard field goal with 45 seconds left and Michigan stunned No. 2 Ohio State 13-10, ending the Buckeyes’ hopes of returning to the Big Ten title game.
item.dc:creator: Mitch Stacy, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Josh Allen's ex-girlfriend claims account was hacked after comment mocking CTE
item.description: Buffalo Bills quarterback Josh Allen's ex-girlfriend deleted a comment and a post from her account after news of Allen's engagement.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US and England women draw 0-0 in Emma Hayes' homecoming
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Emma Hayes oversaw a dominant display from her United States team at a packed Wembley Stadium without tasting victory on her return to England on Saturday. Hayes, a Londoner who coached the U.S.
item.dc:creator: SAMUEL PETREQUIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Jared Porter acknowledges he sent inappropriate text message to reporter, leading Mets to fire him
item.description: Jared Porter acknowledged he sent an inappropriate text message to a reporter while he was a Chicago Cubs executive in 2016, which led to the New York Mets firing him as general manager in 2021 after just 38 days.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mikaela Shiffrin suffers abrasion on hip during crash on final run of World Cup giant slalom
item.description: American skier Mikaela Shiffrin says she suffered an abrasion on her left hip when crashing during her second run of a World Cup giant slalom race.
item.dc:creator: Peggy Shinn, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: What to know before Cal kicks off against No. 9 SMU
item.description: SMU won the only previous meeting, beating a one-win Cal team 13-7 at Berkeley on Sept. 21, 1957. A year later, the Bears played in their most recent Rose Bowl game.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Faraudo, The Mercury News

item.title: Marley magic: Pittsburg defeats San Ramon Valley in NCS Division I title game behind clutch play from quarterback Marley Alcantara
item.description: NCS Division I football championship: Pittsburg quarterback Marley Alcantara leads Pittsburg to fourth consecutive Division I title with 35-28 win over San Ramon Valley.
item.dc:creator: Nathan Canilao, The Mercury News

item.title: USU wins NIT season tip-off over North Texas, 61-57
item.description: ORLANDO (ABC4 Sports) - A career-best scoring performance from Mason Falslev helped lead Utah State men’s basketball (7-0, 0-0 MW) to a championship game title at the NIT Season Tip-Off Friday evening, as the Aggies defeated North Texas 61-57 at the ESPN Wide World of Sports Complex.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Colorado Buffaloes finishes season with a win over Oklahoma State 52-0
item.description: GRAND JUNCTION, Colo. (KREX)—Colorado head coach Deion Sanders confidently told the media before the game that his team wasn’t just aiming to win but to dominate. Travis Hunter wasted no time backing up that claim.
item.dc:creator: Titus Cleveland, KREX 5

item.title: Colorado State advances to MW volleyball final and will take the court against San Jose State
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — Colorado State coach Emily Kohan said her team will take the floor rather than become the latest team to forfeit against San Jose State after the top-seeded Rams advanced to the Mountain West volleyball tournament final on Friday.
item.dc:creator: MARK ANDERSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Spurgeon breaks 33-game scoreless streak with two goals, Minnesota beats Chicago 3-2
item.description: ST. PAUL, Minn. (AP) — Jared Spurgeon ended a 33-game scoring drought with two of Minnesota’s three second-period goals and the Wild beat the Chicago Blackhawks 3-2 on Friday. Marco Rossi also scored for Minnesota and Marc-Andre Fleury, who turned 40 on Thursday, made 20 saves to improve to 5-0-1.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Chiefs narrowly escape another lowly opponent with bizarre ending against Raiders to clinch playoff spot
item.description: The Kansas City Chiefs again narrowly squeaked by an inferior opponent, and this victory, which got them into the playoffs, was bizarre.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Coach Deion Sanders says No. 23 Colorado's best players will play in bowl game
item.description: BOULDER, Colo. (AP) — Colorado quarterback Shedeur Sanders says he'll play in his team's bowl game, while his dad says he's planning on CU's Heisman favorite, Travis Hunter, being there, too.
item.dc:creator: EDDIE PELLS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Chicago Bears fire head coach Matt Eberflus after disastrous Thanksgiving loss
item.description: The Chicago Bears have fired head coach Matt Eberflus following a six-game losing streak, with offensive coordinator Thomas Brown serving as interim coach.
item.dc:creator: Marisa Rodriguez, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Chicago looks to break Boston's 6-game win streak
item.description: Boston Celtics (15-3, second in the Eastern Conference) vs. Chicago Bulls (8-12, 10th in the Eastern Conference) Chicago; Friday, 8 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: San Jose State is disappointed Boise State forfeited but looks forward to volleyball final
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — San Jose State said Thursday it was disappointed Boise State forfeited its semifinal match in the Mountain West women's volleyball tournament but that the Spartans were focused on the future.
item.dc:creator: MARK ANDERSON, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Depleted Jazz lose to Denver, 122-103
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - Without injured stars Lauri Markkanen, John Collins and Jordan Clarkson, the Utah Jazz had little chance against the Denver Nuggets and lost for the sixth time in their last seven games.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Real Salt Lake makes 2024 season-end roster decisions
item.description: Real Salt Lake has revealed its end-of-season roster decision following a record-setting 2024 campaign in Major League Soccer.
item.dc:creator: Mattey Casey, ABC 4

item.title: Sergachev scores in overtime in 3-2 Utah HC win
item.description: MONTREAL (ABC4 Sports) - Mikhail Sergachev scored with 27 seconds remaining in overtime to give the Utah Hockey Club a 3-2 win against the Montreal Canadiens at Bell Centre on Tuesday. Sergachev directed a Nick Schmaltz pass through the crease inside the left post.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Rays will play 19 of their first 22 games at home as MLB switches series to avoid summer rain
item.description: Major League Baseball switched a pair of series involving the Tampa Bay Rays to the first two months of the season in an attempt to avoid summer rain at open-air Steinbrenner Field, their temporary home following damage to Tropicana Field.
item.dc:creator: AP, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Chiefs' Patrick Mahomes fined over $14K for 'violent gesture' at Bills game
item.description: The star quarterback celebrated a touchdown pass with a hand gesture that appeared to imitate him holding a gun.
item.dc:creator: Olivia Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: St. Louis Blues fire Drew Bannister and hire Jim Montgomery as coach
item.description: Drew Bannister is out as coach of the St. Louis Blues after less than a full calendar year on the job. Jim Montgomery is back behind the bench of an NHL team five days after he was fired by the Boston Bruins.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN WHYNO, Associated Press, Fox 5
